feed.title: SkillsLLM
feed.subtitle: AI tooling news and guides: SkillsLLM, Desktop Commander, Model Context Protocol, FastMCP, ClaudePluginHub, MCP Servers blog, Claude Skills Hub, OpenRouter, LiteLLM (blog + releases), Glama (blog, MCP servers, release notes), LobeHub (changelog + blog), and AI Skill Market

entry.title: Merchant of Record, Explained: Paddle vs. Stripe
entry.content: Stripe processes payments; Paddle becomes the legal seller. For a solo founder selling digital products across borders, that distinction decides who handles VAT, GST, refunds, and chargebacks — you or them.

entry.title: Why Stripe Won't Let You Take Payments Yet
entry.content: You created a Stripe account, sent a payment link, and hit 'your account cannot currently make live charges.' Here's what actually activates live mode — and the shortest path from zero to a real first dollar.

entry.title: Pillar Pages and Content Clusters for AI Citation
entry.content: Digital Applied found 86% of AI citations come from sites with 5+ interlinked pieces on one topic. Yext found a bidirectionally-linked cluster gets cited 2.7x more than an isolated page. AI crawlers read internal links as a map — here's how to structure content as one knowledge node.

entry.title: Ten Schema Tags That Get You Cited by AI
entry.content: Schema markup alone does nothing — Ahrefs tested 1,885 pages and adding it with no content change produced no citation lift. But Organization and FAQPage JSON-LD on top of real content earn 2.7x more AI citations. Here's the code and the deployment checklist.

entry.title: The Reddit Playbook for Solo Founders Who Hate Marketing
entry.content: Reddit is Perplexity's #1 source at 46.7% of top citations and #1 in Google AI Overviews at 21%. One genuinely useful 30-minute answer keeps getting pulled into AI responses indefinitely. Here's the minimal-effort playbook — and why this window won't stay open.

entry.title: Why AI Engines Cite Reddit Over Your Homepage
entry.content: AI engines trust what others say about you far more than what you say about yourself. Muck Rack found 82% of AI citations come from earned media, under 6% from brand-owned pages. The real work of GEO isn't polishing your copy — it's getting mentioned elsewhere.

entry.title: Search Traffic Forked: SEO and GEO Are Different Games
entry.content: Search just split into two paths: the ranked blue-link list and the AI-generated answer that quotes a few sources nobody clicks. SEO races for rank; GEO races to be the source the AI draws from. Here's why the distinction reorders how solo founders get found.

entry.title: The Compliance Deadline That Costs $25,000 If You Miss It
entry.content: Most 'form a US LLC' guides stop at the EIN. The part they skip can cost $25,000: Form 5472, required annually from foreign-owned LLCs even at zero revenue. Plus the BOI rule that changed in 2025 and the state report you still owe.

entry.title: Getting an EIN Without a Social Security Number
entry.content: No SSN? You can still get an EIN by phone or fax — the widely-repeated claim that the IRS killed the fax channel is wrong. Here's the SS-4 prep that keeps you from getting rejected: Line 7b, Line 11, Line 15, and the Responsible Party rule.

entry.title: Wyoming vs. Delaware vs. California for a Solo LLC
entry.content: Why an LLC beats a C-Corp for most solo founders, and which state to form in. Wyoming's $60-a-year default, Delaware's investor pull, and California's $800 franchise-tax trap — plus the registered-agent requirement nobody explains until you're stuck.

entry.title: Give Your AI Agent Legs: Control It From Your Phone
entry.content: The real payoff of a personal mesh VPN: run an AI coding agent on a home machine and drive it from your phone while the task keeps running. Here's the latency math, the security details that matter, and the one hard limit to plan around.

entry.title: Access Your Home Server From Anywhere, No Open Ports
entry.content: Tailscale turns every device you own into one private network — no public IP, no firewall rules, no forwarded ports. Here's the connection model that makes it work, and the one thing that quietly leaves you stuck on relay latency.

entry.title: Build a Waitlist Page in 30 Minutes, Zero Servers
entry.content: A concrete 6-step path from empty folder to a live email-capture page — frontend, backend, and database — deployed worldwide on Cloudflare for $0, before a single line of the real product exists. The fastest way to test if anyone wants the thing.

entry.title: Deploy Global Infrastructure for Free on Cloudflare
entry.content: Cloudflare Workers and Pages replace 'rent a VPS and configure nginx' with 'push code, it runs in 335 cities.' Here's why serverless-at-the-edge is a different model, where the free tier ceiling actually sits, and how Pages and Workers are the same primitive.

entry.title: Keeping Your Free Oracle VPS From Getting Reclaimed
entry.content: Oracle's free tier has one real string attached: it reclaims idle instances. Here's what 'idle' actually means for a dev box, the keep-alive pattern that works, why community scripts are a fragile bet, and the fix that ends the problem for good.

entry.title: Oracle's Always-Free Tier: The Server Everyone Forgets
entry.content: Oracle Cloud quietly gives away a permanent 4-core, 24GB-RAM ARM server that would cost $80-100/month on AWS. Here's why signups get rejected, the region choice you can't undo, and how to actually get the machine.

entry.title: Your First US Bank Card Without Setting Foot in America
entry.content: The four-piece kit a non-resident founder needs before a US bank says yes: ITIN, a CMRA-clean address, a US SIM, and a Wise account, plus the card application.

entry.title: The ITIN Path: A US Tax ID Without a US Address
entry.content: What an ITIN is, why a Certifying Acceptance Agent beats mailing your passport to the IRS, the 8-12 week timeline, and why it's the first US-entity domino.

entry.title: Ship a Static Site With Nothing but Git Push
entry.content: Why GitHub Pages beats Vercel, Netlify, and a VPS for a solo founder's first landing page: zero cost, no third-party login, and the deploy is a git push.

entry.title: Domain Name to Live DNS in Twenty Minutes
entry.content: Registrar tradeoffs, why to route DNS through Cloudflare, the DNSSEC gotcha that takes domains offline, and the orange-vs-grey cloud rule for GitHub Pages.

entry.title: The Questions Every Teacher Asks Before Signing Up
entry.content: Eligibility, verification, cost, data privacy, and what happens if you change schools — the practical FAQ for ChatGPT for Teachers, and a look back at the series.

entry.title: What Happens When the Free Period Ends in 2027
entry.content: ChatGPT for Teachers is free only through June 2027. OpenAI hasn't said what comes next. Here's what's known, what's speculation, and what to plan for regardless.

entry.title: No, Students Don't Get Free ChatGPT Too
entry.content: ChatGPT for Teachers is a teacher-and-staff offer, not a student one — and several big districts ban student ChatGPT outright. Untangling the confusion.

entry.title: Choosing a Pilot: A Decision Guide for Districts
entry.content: ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, or Copilot for your district AI pilot? A practical framework built on the stack you already run, real per-seat prices, and priorities.

entry.title: Building the Skill a Free Chatbot Can't Replace
entry.content: A free, general teacher assistant is genuinely useful — and structurally incapable of three things a purpose-built skill does. Here's the line between them.

entry.title: Where AI Tutoring Skills Fit Between the Giants
entry.content: ChatGPT for Teachers and Claude for Teachers are both teacher-facing productivity tools. The student-facing Socratic tutor is a niche neither giant is actually filling.

entry.title: What Claude Skill Builders Can Learn From OpenAI
entry.content: OpenAI's education playbook — narrow vertical, distribution through a trusted intermediary, free to build the habit — is a GTM lesson for anyone building a Claude Code skill.

entry.title: The Squeeze on Third-Party Classroom AI Tools
entry.content: Paid lesson-planning and differentiation tools now compete with something free from the model vendor itself. What the squeeze looks like, and which tools escape it.

entry.title: What a Free Vertical AI Product Means for EdTech
entry.content: When the model vendor ships a free, polished, education-specific product directly to teachers, the ecosystem built on its general-purpose API has to rethink what it's for.

entry.title: Piloting ChatGPT for Teachers District-Wide
entry.content: A free tool still costs you a rollout. How to pilot ChatGPT for Teachers across a district — cohort selection, a real feedback loop, and the scale-or-stop decision.

entry.title: Role-Based Access Control for School Workspaces
entry.content: Who should be an admin, who should be a member, and the RBAC misconfigurations that quietly hand a whole district's AI workspace to the wrong people. A practical guide for K-12 IT.

entry.title: Reading the Student Data Privacy Agreement
entry.content: OpenAI names itself a FERPA 'School Official' and says student data stays under the school's control. Here's what those terms actually commit — and what a privacy officer should still ask.

entry.title: SSO, MFA, and the District IT Rollout Plan
entry.content: The mechanics of a managed ChatGPT for Teachers deployment: claim the domain, wire up SAML SSO, enforce MFA, and onboard teachers in phases without flooding the help desk.

entry.title: A Procurement Checklist for ChatGPT for Teachers
entry.content: It's free, so districts skip procurement. That's the mistake. Here's the checklist an IT director should run before ChatGPT for Teachers reaches a single classroom.

entry.title: When Voice Mode Beats Typing in the Classroom
entry.content: Voice mode isn't a gimmick for teachers. It wins in the exact moments a classroom leaves your hands full and your keyboard out of reach. Here's when to talk instead of type.

entry.title: Image Generation for Worksheets and Slides
entry.content: How to use ChatGPT for Teachers' image generation for worksheets, slides, and classroom visuals — and why every AI-generated diagram needs an accuracy check first.

entry.title: Differentiation and IEP Support, Carefully Done
entry.content: How to use ChatGPT for Teachers to draft differentiated materials and IEP-aligned supports — and exactly where FERPA caution around student-identifying data has to stop you.

entry.title: Writing Parent Emails Without the Dread
entry.content: How ChatGPT for Teachers helps draft difficult parent emails — setting tone, structuring hard news, and translating — with the rule to always personalize and review first.

entry.title: Turning File Uploads Into Faster Grading Prep
entry.content: How file uploads in ChatGPT for Teachers speed up rubric-building and feedback drafting — without letting AI grade for you or auto-send anything to students.

entry.title: Co-Planning Lessons in Shared Projects
entry.content: How ChatGPT for Teachers' shared projects turn department co-planning from a Friday scramble into a durable, shared workspace. A realistic PLC workflow.

entry.title: Building a Custom GPT for Your Department
entry.content: A custom GPT turns a good prompt into a reusable tool your whole department shares. Here's how to build a rubric-grader or differentiation GPT that actually gets used.

entry.title: The Four-Way AI Vendor Race for the Classroom
entry.content: OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, and Microsoft are all racing for the same classrooms — with four different strategies. Here's how the board actually looks.

entry.title: Why OpenAI Chose Free Over a Freemium Tier
entry.content: OpenAI could have shipped a discounted teacher tier. It shipped a fully free one through June 2027. The go-to-market logic behind that choice is worth reading.

entry.title: Context Windows, Compared for Classroom Use
entry.content: Token context sounds abstract until you try uploading a whole class set of essays. Here's what 400K vs 1M–2M tokens actually means for a teacher's day.

entry.title: The Real Price of "Free": A Cost Comparison
entry.content: ChatGPT for Teachers is free per seat — but adoption isn't. Here's the true cost of rolling it out versus paid Gemini, Copilot, and Claude for schools.

entry.title: ChatGPT for Teachers vs Microsoft Copilot in Class
entry.content: Same GPT-5.1 engine, different chassis. A free standalone workspace versus an Office-native paid tier — and why the model isn't what decides this one.

entry.title: Gemini for Education: The Google Gravity Case
entry.content: For a Google-shop district, the AI question isn't which model is smarter. It's whether Workspace gravity and NotebookLM beat a free standalone ChatGPT.

entry.title: Claude for Education: The Other Free Offer
entry.content: OpenAI isn't the only company giving teachers AI for free. Anthropic's education play — a free K-12 offer plus a paid, partner-rich tier — is the quieter alternative.

entry.title: The Conflict-of-Interest Question in AI Training
entry.content: AI vendors are funding the union that trains teachers to use their tools. That's a real conflict worth naming — and the safeguards deserve a fair hearing too.

entry.title: Free for Teachers, Banned for Students: The Split
entry.content: OpenAI is courting teachers with a free workspace while districts ban ChatGPT for students. Is that a contradiction — or two different problems wearing one name?

entry.title: Oregon Teachers Are Calling AI Deals a "Grift"
entry.content: An Oregon NEA rank-and-file caucus wants an end to what it calls 'AI grift.' Here's what that grassroots pushback represents — and what it doesn't, within a mixed union landscape.

entry.title: What the MIT Cognitive Engagement Study Found
entry.content: An MIT study found students who used ChatGPT to write essays showed weaker cognitive engagement. Here's what that implies for student use — and why teacher use is a different case.

entry.title: Baltimore, LA, NYC, Seattle: Why They Banned ChatGPT
entry.content: Major districts banned ChatGPT over integrity and quality fears. Now OpenAI is courting those same districts with free teacher accounts. Are the two positions contradictory?

entry.title: Why Detection Tools Miss 1 in 5 AI Essays
entry.content: AI-writing detectors run roughly 80-90% accurate — and most don't publish rates at all. Here's why that's not good enough to be a teacher's primary defense.

entry.title: The Cheating Numbers Behind ChatGPT for Teachers
entry.content: A Study.com survey found over a quarter of teachers have caught a student cheating with ChatGPT. Here's what that number actually measures — and what it doesn't.

entry.title: Can a Course Make 400,000 Teachers AI-Literate
entry.content: The AFT's academy promises to train 400,000 teachers on AI in five years. But scale and depth pull against each other — can standardized training build real literacy?

entry.title: ChatGPT Foundations for Teachers, Reviewed
entry.content: A fair critical review of OpenAI's free teacher course — what it teaches well, and the gaps around AI grading, hallucination, and detection it glosses over.

entry.title: Inside the AI Skills Jam for K-12 Educators
entry.content: OpenAI's AI Skills Jam took the teacher training on the road — in-person workshops across US cities that drew 1,600+ educators. Here's what the format looks like.

entry.title: What OpenAI's Free Teacher AI Course Covers
entry.content: OpenAI Academy runs two free courses for K-12 teachers: AI Foundations and ChatGPT Foundations for Teachers. Here's the actual syllabus, section by section.

entry.title: Three Rivals, One Classroom: AFT's AI Coalition
entry.content: OpenAI, Microsoft, and Anthropic compete everywhere else — but co-funded the same $23M union program to train 400,000 teachers. Here's why.

entry.title: July Townhall: Product + Roadmap Updates
entry.content: Join the LiteLLM July townhall on Thursday, 23 July at 7:30 AM PT to learn about LiteLLM's product updates and roadmap.

entry.title: emily
entry.content: Emily's personal Claude Code skills

entry.title: react-architecture-plugin
entry.content: React, Next.js, React Router 개발 컨벤션 스킬 모음

entry.title: fig
entry.content: fig — a plugin for Claude Code

entry.title: ralph-loop
entry.content: Continuous self-referential AI loops for interactive iterative development, implementing the Ralph Wiggum technique. Run Claude in a while-true loop with the same prompt until task completion.

entry.title: session-report
entry.content: Generate an explorable HTML report of Claude Code session usage — tokens, cache efficiency, subagents, skills, and the most expensive prompts — from local ~/.claude/projects transcripts.

entry.title: telegram
entry.content: Telegram channel for Claude Code — messaging bridge with built-in access control. Manage pairing, allowlists, and policy via /telegram:access.

entry.title: skill-creator
entry.content: Create new skills, improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, update or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, or benchmark skill performance with variance analysis.

entry.title: rust-analyzer-lsp
entry.content: Rust language server for code intelligence and analysis

entry.title: security-guidance
entry.content: Security review for Claude-generated code. Pattern-based warnings on edits, LLM-powered diff review on Stop, and an agentic commit reviewer that catches injection, XSS, SSRF, hardcoded secrets, and 25+ other vulnerability classes.

entry.title: ruby-lsp
entry.content: Ruby language server for code intelligence and analysis

entry.title: pyright-lsp
entry.content: Python language server (Pyright) for type checking and code intelligence

entry.title: serena
entry.content: Semantic code analysis MCP server providing intelligent code understanding, refactoring suggestions, and codebase navigation through language server protocol integration.

entry.title: terraform
entry.content: The Terraform MCP Server provides seamless integration with Terraform ecosystem, enabling advanced automation and interaction capabilities for Infrastructure as Code (IaC) development.

entry.title: typescript-lsp
entry.content: TypeScript/JavaScript language server for enhanced code intelligence

entry.title: swift-lsp
entry.content: Swift language server (SourceKit-LSP) for code intelligence

entry.title: cypherpoet-swift-xcode-kit
entry.content: Swift and Xcode development kit: SwiftUI best practices and 2027 SDK migration, UIKit multi-window modernization, XCTest-to-Swift-Testing migration, security-hardening audits of Xcode build settings, C -fbounds-safety guidance, and on-device/simulator UI verification.

entry.title: cypherpoet-google-filament-kit
entry.content: Comprehensive working knowledge of Google Filament, the real-time physically-based rendering engine — the PBR material model and lighting/IBL, the material definition language and matc compiler, the core engine API (Engine/Scene/View/Renderer/Camera, resources, gltfio), and per-binding setup for C++, Web (JS/WASM), and Android, distilled from the official documentation with a synced reference corpus that tracks Filament releases.

entry.title: plugin-dev
entry.content: Plugin development toolkit with skills for creating agents, commands, hooks, MCP integrations, and comprehensive plugin structure guidance

entry.title: firebase
entry.content: Google Firebase MCP integration. Manage Firestore databases, authentication, cloud functions, hosting, and storage. Build and manage your Firebase backend directly from your development workflow.

entry.title: cypherpoet-svg-tools
entry.content: SVG optimization and cleanup.

entry.title: cypherpoet-git-hygiene
entry.content: Keep local git state tidy: sync branches with the remote, and clean up stale branches and worktrees with per-item approval.

entry.title: cypherpoet-session-harvest
entry.content: Harvest a session's learnings into their right homes: a memory, a suggested repo edit (CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md, docs, or a hook), or a PR that improves one of your own agent skills.

entry.title: cypherpoet-mobile-dev
entry.content: iOS App Store publishing best practices.

entry.title: gopls-lsp
entry.content: Go language server for code intelligence and refactoring

entry.title: gitlab
entry.content: GitLab DevOps platform integration. Manage repositories, merge requests, CI/CD pipelines, issues, and wikis. Full access to GitLab's comprehensive DevOps lifecycle tools.

entry.title: hookify
entry.content: Easily create hooks to prevent unwanted behaviors by analyzing conversation patterns

entry.title: kotlin-lsp
entry.content: Kotlin language server for code intelligence

entry.title: discord
entry.content: Discord channel for Claude Code — messaging bridge with built-in access control. Manage pairing, allowlists, and policy via /discord:access.

entry.title: playground
entry.content: Creates interactive HTML playgrounds — self-contained single-file explorers with visual controls, live preview, and prompt output with copy button

entry.title: frontend-design
entry.content: Frontend design skill for UI/UX implementation

entry.title: lua-lsp
entry.content: Lua language server for code intelligence

entry.title: cypherpoet-sf-symbols-kit
entry.content: Apple SF Symbols end to end: find the right symbol with natural language, export clean recolorable SVGs at any of the 9 weights, browse an HTML gallery, build full icon sets, and convert your own SVG art into importable custom SF Symbol templates.

entry.title: cypherpoet-git-flow
entry.content: Bundle of git commit and changelog hygiene plugins: emoji commits and changelog maintenance.

entry.title: imessage
entry.content: iMessage channel for Claude Code — reads chat.db directly, sends via AppleScript. Built-in access control; manage pairing, allowlists, and policy via /imessage:access.

entry.title: php-lsp
entry.content: PHP language server (Intelephense) for code intelligence

entry.title: cypherpoet-session-handoff
entry.content: Write a structured handoff document so a fresh agent can resume long-running work without losing context.

entry.title: cypherpoet-marketplace-kit
entry.content: Maintainer toolkit for running a plugin marketplace with Claude Code and Codex catalogs — publish plugins, audit marketplace and catalog sync, and regenerate the local catalog.

entry.title: mcp-server-dev
entry.content: Skills for designing and building MCP servers that work seamlessly with Claude — guides you through deployment models (remote HTTP, MCPB, local), tool design patterns, auth, and interactive MCP apps.

entry.title: math-olympiad
entry.content: Solve competition math (IMO, Putnam, USAMO) with adversarial verification that catches what self-verification misses. Fresh-context verifiers attack proofs with specific failure patterns. Calibrated abstention over bluffing.

entry.title: fakechat
entry.content: Localhost iMessage-style web chat for Claude Code — test surface with file upload and edits. No tokens, no access control.

entry.title: github
entry.content: Official GitHub MCP server for repository management. Create issues, manage pull requests, review code, search repositories, and interact with GitHub's full API directly from Claude Code.

entry.title: playwright
entry.content: Browser automation and end-to-end testing MCP server by Microsoft. Enables Claude to interact with web pages, take screenshots, fill forms, click elements, and perform automated browser testing workflows.

entry.title: cypherpoet-threejs-kit
entry.content: Three.js / WebGPU / WebGL tooling.

entry.title: cypherpoet-react-three-fiber-kit
entry.content: React Three Fiber (R3F) + drei tooling for declarative Three.js in React.

entry.title: pr-review-toolkit
entry.content: Comprehensive PR review agents specializing in comments, tests, error handling, type design, code quality, and code simplification

entry.title: learning-output-style
entry.content: Interactive learning mode that requests meaningful code contributions at decision points (mimics the unshipped Learning output style)

entry.title: feature-dev
entry.content: Comprehensive feature development workflow with specialized agents for codebase exploration, architecture design, and quality review

entry.title: laravel-boost
entry.content: Laravel development toolkit MCP server. Provides intelligent assistance for Laravel applications including Artisan commands, Eloquent queries, routing, migrations, and framework-specific code generation.

entry.title: cypherpoet-webgl-kit
entry.content: Raw WebGL2 + GLSL shader tooling.

entry.title: mcp-tunnels
entry.content: Connect Claude to a private MCP server through an Anthropic MCP tunnel. Drives the Docker Compose quickstart end to end: certificates, proxy config, cloudflared, and a verifiable sample server.

entry.title: AgentSEO MCP Server
entry.content: AgentSEO MCP Server

entry.content: ipums-mcp

entry.content: Personal Finance MCP Server

entry.title: @y0exchange/mcp
entry.content: @y0exchange/mcp

entry.title: MemoryBuddy
entry.content: MemoryBuddy

entry.title: agent-collab
entry.content: Unified dynamic-host collaboration package with centralized skills, migration preflight, and a verified plugin-relative native runtime boundary. The signed runtime artifact is intentionally absent until the private build/sign integration completes.

entry.title: bm
entry.content: Buildomator: structured workflow plugin for Claude Code with planning, execution, verification, and MCP-backed project state

entry.title: spine
entry.content: Set of skills, agents, and guardrails to complement the Spine AI platform.

entry.title: locus
entry.content: A private chief-of-staff workspace. Everything filed. Nothing forgotten.

entry.title: onecodex
entry.content: One Codex skills: workflow authoring and Python client quick reference

entry.title: create-pr
entry.content: Open a GitHub pull request for the current branch, formatted to your team's PR convention. Infers that convention from your recent PRs on first run. Jira integration is optional. Requires the gh CLI, plus jira in Jira mode.

entry.title: flowleap-tools
entry.content: FlowLeap CLI tool pack — the flowleap-* skills that teach an agent to drive the FlowLeap CLI and backend facade: authentication, provider keys, EPO/USPTO/OPS patent search, academic and non-patent literature, legal references, citations, and the unified /v1/tools facade.

entry.title: android-unit-testing
entry.content: Generate and review unit tests for Clean Architecture + MVVM Android apps (Repository / UseCase / ViewModel) targeting ≥90% line coverage. Detects the project's mocking library, JUnit version, and error model; supports MockK and Mockito.

entry.title: workflow-observer
entry.content: 로컬 coding-agent workflow를 관찰하고 근거 기반 개선안을 제안하는 plugin

entry.title: recipes
entry.content: FlowLeap patent-research recipe pack — end-to-end workflows for prior-art search, freedom-to-operate, patent landscape, claim analysis, patent-to-report, and academic literature review, all driven through the FlowLeap CLI / backend facade.

entry.title: flowline
entry.content: Dataverse ALM CLI — plugin registration, web resources, and solution deploy via Git-tracked, unmanaged solutions. Includes migration guidance off spkl, Daxif, PACX, and ALM Accelerator.

entry.title: citrate-federation
entry.content: Navigation and project-handling skills for the CitrateNetwork federated repositories: orientation sequence, truth hierarchy, manifest/drift discipline, handoffs, and parallel agent dispatch.

entry.title: android-codegen-ui
entry.content: Generate Jetpack Compose UI (screens + ViewModel/UiState/Event/FieldState, reusable components, icons, strings, permissions) from a Figma design for Clean Architecture Android apps. Six-stage pipeline (0–5) with a mandatory human review gate after Plan. Scope is features/presentation only — never domain/data. Requires the Figma MCP server; assumes com.ictechnology.app + Material 3.

entry.title: android-compose-review
entry.content: Code-review orchestrator for Jetpack Compose Android apps (Clean Architecture + MVVM + Hilt). Reviews a diff/branch/PR for correctness bugs and routes Compose/Kotlin idioms to 13 bundled focused skills.

entry.title: personas
entry.content: FlowLeap persona skill pack — patent attorney, IP analyst, researcher, and startup-founder workflows that operate entirely through the FlowLeap CLI / backend facade.

entry.title: eris
entry.content: Work Eris workspaces as a linked frontier agent

entry.title: nacl
entry.content: NaCl framework: BA/SA/TL spec-graph methodology skills, backed by a Neo4j project graph.

entry.title: agentile
entry.content: The Agentile methodology as skills: feature specs, plansets, test planning, red-green work packages, refactor + mutation testing, documentation passes, retrospectives, journals, essays, and case studies.

entry.title: concierge-foundry
entry.content: AI-powered staffing via Azure AI Foundry hosted agents. Parallel talent scouts run server-side — staff opportunities, evaluate lineups, and analyze the pipeline.

entry.title: android-codegen-non-ui
entry.content: Generate the non-UI data layer (Retrofit API → Repository → UseCase, + Room/Paging/DI) for Clean Architecture Android apps from a Scramble/Laravel OpenAPI JSON. Two-stage pipeline with a mandatory human review gate: Plan (plan.yaml + plan-preview.md) then Generate. Assumes the com.ictechnology.core library.

entry.title: rapidocms
entry.content: RapidoCMS : contenu réseaux sociaux (Facebook, Instagram, LinkedIn, TikTok), campagnes de posts, cartes digitales, gestion des marques multi-enseignes et conformité de marque — avec agents responsable-marketing, community-manager, directeur-artistique, prompt-designer et gestionnaire-marques

entry.title: dev-studio
entry.content: A cloud-native software delivery agent team. Seven roles across a Build → Ship → Accept → Promote lifecycle (planner, engineer, reviewer, operator, black-box e2e-tester, marketer, coordinator) and four workflows, building one monorepo derived from github.com/aws300/scaffold. Local-first; the same agents deploy headless to Claude Managed Agents via scripts/cma.

entry.title: rapido-startup
entry.content: Finance & création de startup : interview business plan en 9 phases avec le coach-startup, Stripe (lecture d'abord) croisé avec les 4 serveurs Rapido — trésorerie, unit economics, benchmarks.

entry.title: rapido-direction
entry.content: Chef de cabinet du dirigeant : Gmail, Google Calendar et Google Drive (compte connecté de chacun) unifiés avec CRM, FoodEatUp et n8n — journée du dirigeant, tri de boîte mail en brouillons, secrétariat commercial, coffre documents, délégation du récurrent

entry.title: yaml-frontmatter
entry.content: Validate, add, or update YAML frontmatter on documentation files

entry.title: funzies
entry.content: Fun thinking-style skills and an agent: first-principles breakdowns and What Would Elon Do analysis.

entry.title: tier-layered-teams
entry.content: A two-axis orchestration lattice crossing model-cost team tiers (Opus orchestrator, Sonnet leads, Haiku workers) with T0-T3 tool escalation — Claude Code-native variant.

entry.title: platform-conventions
entry.content: Per-platform house-style conventions: Apple (Swift/SwiftUI), backend (Cloudflare Workers/Hono), web frontend (Next.js), Android/Flutter.

entry.title: rapidorh
entry.content: RapidoRh : projets, Kanban, dailies (rapports journaliers), onboarding des employés et rôles — avec agents chef-de-projet et responsable-rh

entry.title: create-changelog
entry.content: Create or append a dated entry to a skill's CHANGELOG.md

entry.title: rapido-marketing
entry.content: Marketing & acquisition Rapido-first : génération de leads, tunnel de conversion, nurturing, publicité, sales intelligence (transcripts RDV) et analytics — priorité aux serveurs Rapido (CRM/CMS/RH), MCP secondaires en repli.

entry.title: bmad-dream-weaver-agent
entry.content: Dream journaling and interpretation agent with lucid dreaming coaching, pattern discovery, symbol analysis, and recall training.

entry.title: tiered-team-orchestration
entry.content: A three-tier model-cost hierarchy — an Opus-tier core orchestrator plans and routes, Sonnet-tier research/coding/review team leads decompose and synthesize, Haiku-tier workers execute narrow tasks.

entry.title: rhetorical-analysis
entry.content: Analyse rhétorique et épistémologique d'articles, discours et textes argumentatifs.

entry.title: bot-notify
entry.content: Push Claude Code notifications (turn results, human-in-the-loop alerts) to a shared IM chat room. Outbound-only, zero LLM tokens, no Node.js.

entry.title: fix-validation
entry.content: Run scripts/validate.sh, auto-fix mechanical structural issues, and report what needs manual authorship

entry.title: php-code-simplifier
entry.content: Agent that simplifies and refines PHP code for clarity, consistency, and maintainability while preserving functionality

entry.title: paperos-transcript-extractor
entry.content: PaperOS Transcript Extractor for Claude: run the Entity Setup workflow from your onboarding material — drop in a call transcript, notes, or documents and review extracted questionnaire answers in an embedded UI.

entry.title: rapido-canva
entry.content: Design Canva alimenté par les données Rapido : menus restaurant (FoodEatUp), visuels sociaux (RapidoCMS), supports commerciaux (RapidoCRM), présentations CODIR (4 serveurs)

entry.title: scratch
entry.content: Start-from-scratch session utilities: /scratch:fresh rescans ~/.claude (skills, plugins, agents, commands, MCP) so a new session in the current directory knows everything available; /scratch:takeover finds another directory's Claude session, absorbs its history into the current context, moves its files here, and continues the work.

entry.title: wiki-toolkit
entry.content: Skills for maintaining a Karpathy-style LLM wiki: ingest knowledge, look it up, run checkpoints, and lint the wiki.

entry.title: php-lspx
entry.content: PHP language server: intelephense and phpactor multiplexed via lspx for completion, diagnostics, and refactoring.

entry.title: agent-team
entry.content: A domain-agnostic agent-team scaffold built around the Planner → Generator → Evaluator loop. Reusable role agents and a reference end-to-end workflow, with plugin-level hooks, MCP/LSP registration, monitors, output style, and user config. Local-first; the same agents deploy headless to Claude Managed Agents via scripts/cma.

entry.title: hub-and-spoke-orchestration
entry.content: Multi-agent software-engineering pipeline: a central Orchestrator dispatches Planner/Explorer/Coder/Reviewer/Arbiter/Executor spokes and a Scribe write-path service against one shared protocol.

entry.title: rapido-n8n
entry.content: Automatisations n8n sur l'instance du client : usine à workflows (cycle SDK complet), recettes métier Rapido prêtes à déployer, surveillance des exécutions, mémoire opérationnelle — tâche ponctuelle = Claude, tâche récurrente = workflow n8n

entry.title: create-adr
entry.content: Create a new Architecture Decision Record (ADR) in docs/adrs/

entry.title: git-conventions
entry.content: Git workflow skills: Conventional Commit messages and merge request contribution descriptions.

entry.title: bot-chat
entry.content: Bind a Claude Code session to an IM chat room; push long-task results to the room on completion.

entry.title: rapido-forge
entry.content: StartupsForge (PrendsTaPart) : 180 exercices d'incubateur en 3 parcours — bootcamp 5 jours, roadmap idéation, roadmap scale — pilotés par 4 agents (directeur de programme + 3 mentors). Livrables consignés dans ./rapido-kb/startup/forge/, exécution réelle déléguée aux plugins métier Rapido.

entry.title: paperos-entity-setup
entry.content: PaperOS Entity Setup for Claude: form and manage legal entities (LLC, C-Corp, LP, funds) in chat. Bundles the PaperOS connector (formation workflow + knowledge-base tools) with the skills that drive it.

entry.title: DevTools Lens MCP
entry.content: DevTools Lens MCP

entry.title: carta-cap-table
entry.content: Carta Cap Table plugin — skills and hooks for querying cap tables, grants, SAFEs, 409A valuations, waterfall scenarios, and more

entry.title: codspeed
entry.content: CodSpeed plugin for Claude Code helping with performance measurement and optimization.

entry.title: slopweaver
entry.content: Build a local-first, zero-key org world model from your GitHub history — ingest it, tier it (bronze→silver→gold), and ask questions of it, all on your machine.

entry.title: getaway
entry.content: Plan award trips from Claude Code. getaway sweeps seats.aero across 28 mileage programs, composes whole journeys — flights out and home, hotel award nights via rooms.aero — and judges every option like you would: seat quality, layovers, status earning, cash-fare anomalies.

entry.title: cairn
entry.content: Milestone-driven development workflow and tracking system for Claude Code projects: a language-agnostic core with per-repo toolchain profiles (R, Python, Docker image, or generic), plan/implement/review skills, hotfix and release paths, Fable review briefs, and a self-auditing cairn/ file system.

entry.title: redpanda-connect
entry.content: Interactive YAML config and Bloblang authoring for Redpanda Connect

entry.title: bamboohr-automation
entry.content: Automate BambooHR tasks via Rube MCP (Composio): employees, time-off, benefits, dependents, employee updates.

entry.title: hookify
entry.content: Easily create hooks to prevent unwanted behaviors by analyzing conversation patterns

entry.title: skill-creator
entry.content: Provides guidance for creating effective Claude Skills that extend capabilities with specialized knowledge, workflows, and tool integrations.

entry.title: developer-growth-analysis
entry.content: Analyzes your recent Claude Code chat history to identify coding patterns, development gaps, and areas for improvement, curates relevant learning resources from HackerNews, and automatically sends a personalized growth report to your Slack DMs.

entry.title: security-guidance
entry.content: Security reminder hook that warns about potential security issues when editing files, including command injection, XSS, and unsafe code patterns

entry.title: document-skills-docx
entry.content: Skills for working with Microsoft Word documents including creation, editing, and formatting using DOCX format.

entry.title: document-skills-xlsx
entry.content: Skills for working with Excel spreadsheets including creation, editing, formulas, and data manipulation.

entry.title: supabase
entry.content: Supabase MCP integration for database operations, authentication, storage, and real-time subscriptions. Manage your Supabase projects, run SQL queries, and interact with your backend directly.

entry.title: template-skill
entry.content: A template skill that demonstrates the structure and format for creating new Claude Skills.

entry.title: learning-output-style
entry.content: Interactive learning mode that requests meaningful code contributions at decision points (mimics the unshipped Learning output style)

entry.title: tailored-resume-generator
entry.content: Analyzes job descriptions and generates tailored resumes that highlight relevant experience, skills, and achievements to maximize interview chances.

entry.title: changelog-generator
entry.content: Automatically creates user-facing changelogs from git commits by analyzing history and transforming technical commits into customer-friendly release notes.

entry.title: code-review
entry.content: Automated code review for pull requests using multiple specialized agents with confidence-based scoring

entry.title: llm-application-dev
entry.content: LLM application development with LangGraph, RAG systems, vector search, and AI agent architectures for Claude 4.5 and GPT-5.2

entry.title: airtable-automation
entry.content: Automate Airtable tasks via Rube MCP (Composio): records, bases, tables, fields, views.

entry.title: basecamp-automation
entry.content: Automate Basecamp project management, to-dos, messages, people, and to-do list organization via Rube MCP (Composio).

entry.title: webapp-testing
entry.content: Tests local web applications using Playwright for verifying frontend functionality, debugging UI behavior, and capturing screenshots.

entry.title: playwright
entry.content: Browser automation and end-to-end testing MCP server by Microsoft. Enables Claude to interact with web pages, take screenshots, fill forms, click elements, and perform automated browser testing workflows.

entry.title: explanatory-output-style
entry.content: Adds educational insights about implementation choices and codebase patterns (mimics the deprecated Explanatory output style)

entry.title: claude-code-setup
entry.content: Analyze codebases and recommend tailored Claude Code automations such as hooks, skills, MCP servers, and subagents.

entry.title: frontend-design
entry.content: Frontend design skill for UI/UX implementation

entry.title: mcp-builder
entry.content: Guides creation of high-quality MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers for integrating external APIs and services with LLMs using Python or TypeScript.

entry.title: serena
entry.content: Semantic code analysis MCP server providing intelligent code understanding, refactoring suggestions, and codebase navigation through language server protocol integration.

entry.title: asana-automation
entry.content: Automate Asana tasks via Rube MCP (Composio): tasks, projects, sections, teams, workspaces.

entry.title: plugin-dev
entry.content: Comprehensive toolkit for developing Claude Code plugins. Includes 7 expert skills covering hooks, MCP integration, commands, agents, and best practices. AI-assisted plugin creation and validation.

entry.title: activecampaign-automation
entry.content: Automate ActiveCampaign tasks via Rube MCP (Composio): manage contacts, tags, list subscriptions, automation enrollment, and tasks.

entry.title: amplitude-automation
entry.content: Automate Amplitude tasks via Rube MCP (Composio): events, user activity, cohorts, user identification.

entry.title: code-simplifier
entry.content: Agent that simplifies and refines code for clarity, consistency, and maintainability while preserving functionality

entry.title: github
entry.content: Official GitHub MCP server for repository management. Create issues, manage pull requests, review code, search repositories, and interact with GitHub's full API directly from Claude Code.

entry.title: raffle-winner-picker
entry.content: Randomly selects winners from lists, spreadsheets, or Google Sheets for giveaways and contests with cryptographically secure randomness.

entry.title: playground
entry.content: Creates interactive HTML playgrounds — self-contained single-file explorers with visual controls, live preview, and prompt output with copy button

entry.title: ralph-loop
entry.content: Continuous self-referential AI loops for interactive iterative development, implementing the Ralph Wiggum technique. Run Claude in a while-true loop with the same prompt until task completion.

entry.title: claude-md-management
entry.content: Tools to maintain and improve CLAUDE.md files - audit quality, capture session learnings, and keep project memory current.

entry.title: skill-creator
entry.content: Create new skills, improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, update or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, or benchmark skill performance with variance analysis.

entry.title: feature-dev
entry.content: Comprehensive feature development workflow with specialized agents for codebase exploration, architecture design, and quality review

entry.title: document-skills-pdf
entry.content: Skills for working with PDF documents including creation, editing, forms, and advanced PDF operations.

entry.title: invoice-organizer
entry.content: Automatically organizes invoices and receipts for tax preparation by reading files, extracting information, and renaming consistently.

entry.title: document-skills-pptx
entry.content: Skills for working with PowerPoint presentations including creation, editing, and formatting using PPTX format.

entry.title: file-organizer
entry.content: Intelligently organizes files and folders by understanding context, finding duplicates, and suggesting better organizational structures.

entry.title: greptile
entry.content: AI code review agent for GitHub and GitLab. View and resolve Greptile's PR review comments directly from Claude Code.

entry.title: corporate-legal
entry.content: Runs M&A diligence at scale with cited tabular review, builds disclosure schedules and closing checklists, drafts board consents and minutes in house format, and tracks entity compliance deadlines across jurisdictions.

entry.title: pyright-lsp
entry.content: Python language server (Pyright) for type checking and code intelligence

entry.title: csharp-lsp
entry.content: C# language server for code intelligence

entry.title: ai-governance-legal
entry.content: Triages proposed AI use cases against your registry, runs impact assessments across the regimes in scope, reviews vendor AI terms for training-on-data and liability gaps, and keeps your AI policy current with practice.

entry.title: brightdata-pack
entry.content: Claude Code skill pack for Bright Data (18 skills)

entry.title: gh-dash
entry.content: GitHub PR dashboard for Claude Code. View PR status, CI/CD progress, bot comments, and merge PRs directly from your terminal.

entry.content: B12 Website Generator plugin allows you to create a professional, engaging, and user-friendly website in seconds using AI. To create a website, you need to provide a name for your project/business, along with a description of the project/business (goals, structure, etc.).

entry.title: code-documentation

entry.title: conjure
entry.content: Intelligent delegation framework for routing tasks to external LLM services while retaining strategic oversight

entry.title: conserve
entry.content: Resource optimization and performance monitoring toolkit for efficient Claude Code workflows

entry.title: agile-product-owner
entry.content: Agile product ownership for backlog management and sprint execution. User story generation (INVEST-compliant), acceptance criteria patterns (Given/When/Then, rule-based, checklist), epic breakdown with 5 split techniques, sprint planning with velocity-based capacity math, and weighted backlog prioritization. Includes user_story_generator Python tool.

entry.title: cuopt-numerical-optimization-formulation
entry.content: Numerical optimization (LP, MILP, QP) — concepts, problem-text parsing, and formulation patterns. What LP, MILP, and QP are, required formulation questions, modeling elements, common patterns, and how to parse problem statements (parameters, constraints, decisions, objective). Domain concepts; no API or interface.

entry.title: attune
entry.content: Full-cycle project development - brainstorm ideas, create specifications, plan architecture, initialize projects, and execute implementation with integrated workflows from superpowers and spec-kit

entry.title: bmad-method-lifecycle
entry.content: Full-lifecycle AI development framework — agents and workflows for product analysis, planning, architecture, and implementation.

entry.title: imbue
entry.content: Intelligent workflow methodologies: analysis, evidence gathering, structured output, and research-enriched feature review with RICE/WSJF/Kano scoring

entry.title: full-stack-orchestration
entry.content: End-to-end feature orchestration with testing, security, performance, and deployment

entry.title: testing
entry.content: Comprehensive testing utilities for operators and applications

entry.title: container-image
entry.content: Container image inspection and analysis using skopeo and podman

entry.title: google-workspace-cli
entry.content: Google Workspace administration via the gws CLI. Install, authenticate, and automate Gmail, Drive, Sheets, Calendar, Docs, Chat, and Tasks. 5 Python tools, 3 reference guides, 43 built-in recipes, 10 persona bundles.

entry.title: business-growth-skills
entry.content: 4 business & growth skills: customer success manager, sales engineer, revenue operations, and contract & proposal writer. Agent skill and plugin for Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI, Cursor, OpenClaw.

entry.title: skill-security-auditor
entry.content: Security audit and vulnerability scanner for AI agent skills. Scans for malicious patterns, prompt injection, data exfiltration, and unsafe file operations.

entry.title: impeccable
entry.content: Design fluency for frontend development. 1 skill with 23 commands (/impeccable polish, /impeccable audit, /impeccable critique, etc.) and curated anti-pattern detection.

entry.title: fullstack-engineer
entry.content: Full-stack engineering with React, Node, databases, and deployment.

entry.title: bigquery
entry.content: BigQuery cost analysis and optimization utilities

entry.title: pw
entry.content: Production-grade Playwright testing toolkit. Generate tests from specs, fix flaky failures, migrate from Cypress/Selenium, sync with TestRail, run on BrowserStack. 55+ ready-to-use templates, 3 specialized agents, smart reporting that plugs into your existing workflow.

entry.title: origin
entry.content: Helpers for openshift/origin development.

entry.title: product-manager
entry.content: Product management toolkit with RICE scoring, customer interview analysis, and PRD generation.

entry.title: terraform-patterns
entry.content: Terraform infrastructure-as-code agent skill and plugin for module design patterns, state management strategies, provider configuration, security hardening, and CI/CD plan/apply workflows. Covers mono-repo vs multi-repo, workspaces, policy-as-code, and drift detection.

entry.title: demand-gen
entry.content: Multi-channel demand generation, paid media optimization, SEO strategy, and partnership programs.

entry.title: si
entry.content: Self-Improving Agent: curate auto-memory, promote learnings to CLAUDE.md and rules, extract proven patterns into reusable skills. Provides /si:review, /si:promote, /si:extract, /si:status, and /si:remember slash commands.

entry.title: workspaces
entry.content: Manage isolated git worktree workspaces for multi-repo development

entry.title: node
entry.content: Kubernetes and OpenShift node health monitoring and diagnostics

entry.title: marketplace-ops
entry.content: Maintenance commands for Claude Code plugin marketplaces

entry.title: ai-sbom
entry.content: Generate AI Software Bill of Materials (SBOM) declarations for PR descriptions

entry.title: aws-architect
entry.content: AWS serverless architecture design with IaC templates, cost optimization, and CI/CD pipelines.

entry.title: console
entry.content: OpenShift Console dynamic plugin development utilities

entry.title: code-tour
entry.content: Create CodeTour .tour files — persona-targeted, step-by-step walkthroughs that link to real files and line numbers. Supports 10 developer personas (vibecoder, new joiner, architect, security reviewer, etc.), all CodeTour step types, and SMIG description formula.

entry.title: cuopt-user-rules
entry.content: Base rules for end users calling NVIDIA cuOpt (routing/LP/MILP/QP/install/server). Not for cuOpt internals — use cuopt-developer for those.

entry.title: helm-chart-builder
entry.content: Helm chart development agent skill and plugin for Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI, Cursor, OpenClaw — chart scaffolding, values design, template patterns, dependency management, security hardening, and chart testing.

entry.title: scrum-master
entry.content: Sprint health analysis, velocity tracking, and retrospective facilitation.

entry.title: code-review
entry.content: Automated code quality review with language-aware analysis for pre-commit verification

entry.title: autoresearch-agent
entry.content: Autonomous experiment loop that optimizes any file by a measurable metric. 5 slash commands, 8 evaluators, configurable loop intervals (10min to monthly).

entry.title: agenthub
entry.content: Multi-agent collaboration plugin for Claude Code. Spawn N parallel subagents that compete on code optimization, content drafts, research approaches, or any problem that benefits from diverse solutions. Evaluate by metric or LLM judge, merge the winner. 7 slash commands, agent templates, git DAG orchestration, message board coordination.

entry.title: content-creator
entry.content: SEO-optimized marketing content with brand voice analysis, content frameworks, and social media templates.

entry.title: docker-development
entry.content: Docker and container development agent skill and plugin for Dockerfile optimization, docker-compose orchestration, multi-stage builds, and container security hardening. Covers build performance, layer caching, and production-ready container patterns.

entry.title: a11y-audit
entry.content: WCAG 2.2 accessibility audit and fix skill for React, Next.js, Vue, Angular, Svelte, and HTML. Static scanner detecting 20+ violation types, contrast checker with suggest mode, framework-specific fix patterns, CI-friendly exit codes.

entry.title: demo-video
entry.content: Create polished demo videos from screenshots and scene descriptions. Orchestrates playwright, ffmpeg, and edge-tts to produce product walkthroughs, feature showcases, and marketing teasers with story structure, scene design system, and narration guidance.

entry.title: rds-analyzer
entry.content: Reference Design Specification (RDS) Analyzer workflow: cluster-compare JSON to deviation reports (text/HTML/reporting) and Jira follow-up

entry.title: code-to-prd
entry.content: Reverse-engineer any codebase into a complete Product Requirements Document (PRD). Analyzes routes, components, models, APIs, and interactions for frontend (React, Vue, Angular, Next.js), backend (NestJS, Django, Express, FastAPI), and fullstack applications.

entry.title: gwapi
entry.content: Gateway API management for Kubernetes/OpenShift clusters

entry.title: golang
entry.content: Go development tools: gopls MCP server, LSP integration, automatic gofmt formatting, golangci-lint linting, and CVE dependency patching

entry.title: ote-migration
entry.content: Automate OpenShift Tests Extension (OTE) migration for component repositories

entry.title: executive-mentor
entry.content: Adversarial thinking partner for founders and executives. Stress-tests plans, prepares for board meetings, navigates hard decisions, and forces honest post-mortems.

entry.title: finance-skills
entry.content: 3 finance skills: financial analyst (ratio analysis, DCF valuation, budgeting, forecasting), SaaS metrics coach (ARR, MRR, churn, CAC, LTV, NRR, Quick Ratio, 12-month projections), and business investment advisor. 7 Python automation tools. Agent skill and plugin for Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI, Cursor, OpenClaw.

entry.title: data-quality-auditor
entry.content: Audit datasets for completeness, consistency, accuracy, and validity. 3 stdlib-only Python tools: data profiler with DQS scoring, missing value analyzer with MCAR/MAR/MNAR classification, and multi-method outlier detector.

entry.title: research-summarizer
entry.content: Structured research summarization agent skill and plugin for Claude Code, Codex, and Gemini CLI. Summarize academic papers, compare web articles, extract citations, and produce actionable research briefs.

entry.title: jira
entry.content: A plugin to automate tasks with Jira

entry.title: dotnet-test
entry.content: Skills for running, diagnosing, and migrating .NET tests: test execution, filtering, platform detection, and MSTest workflows.

entry.title: idealog-builder
entry.content: Turn ideas into projects: autonomous builder and idea decomposition

entry.title: dotnet-diag
entry.content: Skills for .NET performance investigations, debugging, and incident analysis.

entry.title: dotnet-nuget
entry.content: NuGet and .NET package management: dependency management and modernization.

entry.title: dotnet-template-engine
entry.content: .NET Template Engine skills: template discovery, project scaffolding, and template authoring.

entry.title: employment-legal
entry.content: Reviews hires and terminations for jurisdiction-specific risk flags, classifies workers against the controlling state test, tracks leave deadlines before they're missed, runs internal investigations, and drafts policies with state supplements where the law differs.

entry.title: dotnet-upgrade
entry.content: Skills for migrating and upgrading .NET projects across framework versions, language features, and compatibility targets.

entry.title: cuopt-server-common
entry.content: cuOpt REST server — what it does and how requests flow. Domain concepts; no deploy or client code.

entry.title: internal-comms
entry.content: Helps write internal communications including 3P updates, company newsletters, FAQs, status reports, and project updates using company-specific formats.

entry.title: privacy-legal
entry.content: Triages processing activities, generates PIAs, reviews DPAs as controller or processor, drafts DSAR responses within statutory timelines, and monitors policy drift against practice.

entry.title: regulatory-legal
entry.content: Watches regulatory feeds, diffs new rules against your policy library, tracks comment deadlines and open gaps, and writes the digest your team reads Monday morning.

entry.title: brand-guidelines
entry.content: Applies Anthropic's official brand colors and typography to artifacts for consistent visual identity and professional design standards.

entry.title: cocounsel-legal
entry.content: CoCounsel Legal delivers comprehensive Westlaw Deep Research reports with inline, linked citations to Westlaw and Practical Law sources.

entry.title: content-research-writer
entry.content: Assists in writing high-quality content by conducting research, adding citations, improving hooks, and providing section-by-section feedback.

entry.title: dotnet-msbuild
entry.content: Comprehensive MSBuild and .NET build skills: failure diagnosis, performance optimization, code quality, and modernization.

entry.title: dotnet-data
entry.content: Skills for .NET data access and Entity Framework related tasks.

entry.title: dotnet-maui
entry.content: Skills for .NET MAUI development: environment setup, diagnostics, troubleshooting, navigation, data binding, dependency injection, layout, and theming.

entry.title: domain-name-brainstormer
entry.content: Generates creative domain name ideas and checks availability across multiple TLDs including .com, .io, .dev, and .ai extensions.

entry.title: commercial-legal
entry.content: Reviews vendor agreements, NDAs, and SaaS subscriptions against your sales-side or purchasing-side playbook, tracks renewals and cancel-by deadlines before they're missed, routes escalations to the right approver, and translates reviews into summaries business stakeholders will actually read.

entry.title: competitive-ads-extractor
entry.content: Extracts and analyzes competitors' ads from ad libraries to understand messaging and creative approaches that resonate.

entry.title: ip-legal
entry.content: Runs first-pass trademark clearance and freedom-to-operate triage, screens invention disclosures for initial patentability, drafts and triages cease-and-desist letters and DMCA takedowns (send and respond), checks open source compliance, reviews IP clauses, and tracks registrations and renewal deadlines.

entry.title: dotnet-ai
entry.content: AI and ML skills for .NET: technology selection, LLM integration, agentic workflows, RAG pipelines, MCP, and classic ML with ML.NET.

entry.title: litigation-legal
entry.content: Manages the litigation portfolio — matters, deadlines, holds, demands, outside counsel — and does the work: claim charts (patent and civil), chronologies, depo prep, privilege logs, brief drafting. Adapts to how you work litigation: in-house, firm, or solo.

entry.title: legal-builder-hub
entry.content: Finds, evaluates, and installs community legal skills — with a security review gate before anything lands in your environment.

entry.title: meeting-insights-analyzer
entry.content: Analyzes meeting transcripts to uncover behavioral patterns including conflict avoidance, speaking ratios, filler words, and leadership style.

entry.title: law-student
entry.content: Drills Socratically, briefs cases, builds outlines, runs bar prep sessions tuned to your jurisdiction, grades IRAC practice, and plans the study schedule — without ever writing it for you.

entry.title: box-sales-discovery
entry.content: Analyzes Box sales calls using the Discovery Framework with grades on quadrant coverage, decision process mapping, and persona identification for improved deal execution.

entry.title: corporate-legal
entry.content: Runs M&A diligence at scale with cited tabular review, builds disclosure schedules and closing checklists, drafts board consents and minutes in house format, and tracks entity compliance deadlines across jurisdictions.

entry.title: scientific-skills
entry.content: Collection of scientific skills
